ACMCertExpiring7Days.^description: This ACM certificate expires within 7 days. Expired certificates cause HTTPS connection failures for end users. If auto-renewal is not working, renew or replace this certificate immediately.
ACMCertExpiring7Days.shortDesc: Certificate expiring in 7 days — renew immediately

ACMCertExpiring30Days.^description: This ACM certificate expires within 30 days. While ACM auto-renews certificates, renewal can fail if DNS validation records are missing or if the certificate is no longer in use by an AWS service. Verify auto-renewal is working correctly.

ACMCertExpired.^description: This ACM certificate has already expired. Any resources using this certificate are serving HTTPS connections with an invalid certificate, causing browser security warnings and connection failures for users.

ACMCertValidationFailed.^description: Certificate validation has timed out. ACM could not validate domain ownership via DNS or email within the allowed time. The certificate will not be issued until validation is completed.

ACMWildcardCertificate.^description: This is a wildcard certificate. Wildcard certificates cover all subdomains, which means a compromise of the private key affects all subdomains. Use specific domain certificates where possible and store private keys securely.
ACMWildcardCertificate.shortDesc: Review use of wildcard ACM certificate

ACMCertNotInUse.^description: This ACM certificate is not associated with any AWS resource. Unused certificates incur no direct cost but represent unnecessary management overhead. Consider deleting certificates that are no longer needed.

ACMTransparencyLoggingDisabled.^description: Certificate Transparency logging is disabled for this certificate. CT logging is required by major browser vendors — certificates without CT logs may be rejected by browsers with certificate transparency enforcement.
